subject: Mathematics
title: Area and Perimeter
description: A micro-lesson that makes Area and Perimeter usable.

hook.text: A farmer needs to fence a field and plant crops. Two very different measurements matter.

buildup.text: Perimeter measures the boundary. Area measures the space inside. Same shape, different questions.

discovery.text: A rectangle's perimeter is 2(l+w). Its area is l times w. Simple formulas, huge practical value.

twist.text: Shapes with the same perimeter can have wildly different areas. A circle beats them all.

climax.text: This is why bubbles and planets are round: nature maximizes volume for minimum surface.

punchline.text: Nature chose circles because they enclose the most with the least.

quiz.question: Which shape encloses the most area for a given perimeter?
